name: IntuneDeviceInventory
description: A PowerShell module with UI for extending Microsoft Intune device management. Adds custom properties to Intune devices, enables bulk operations including device sync and BitLocker key rotation, with both PowerShell module and standalone UI interfaces.
keywords: how to add custom fields to intune devices
author: Florian Salzmann

securityCheck.aiSummary: Overall assessment: The codebase legitimately implements Intune Graph integration and device inventory features, but contains notable security concerns. Primary risk stems from obfuscated/corrupted module content (IntuneDeviceInventory.psm1 and .psd1) that require immediate integrity verification. Secondary concerns include plaintext secret handling when establishing Graph connections, potential sensitive data collection in get-clientDataDetection.ps1, and reliance on external metadata URLs. Actionable mitigations: verify and restore module files from signed sources, implement secure secret management, minimize and protect collected data, and replace fragile control flow constructs with robust error handling.

noObfuscatedCode.reason: Script module appears as non-human readable with binary-like characters (��<...). This strongly suggests obfuscated or corrupted content. Such packaging can hide malicious payloads and prevents reliable code review. Requires verification against trusted sources and re-extraction from a known-good repository.
